title: The Spirit of the Lord
reading:
The Spirit of the Lord is the first named in Isaiah's sevenfold description — and for good reason. Before strategy, before power, before wisdom, the Spirit of the Lord establishes presence. Everything that flows from the other six spirits begins here, in the reality that God is present, active, and moving.

In Genesis 1:2, the Spirit of God hovered over the formless and void earth. Before a single word of creation was spoken, the Spirit was there — brooding over chaos, preparing for divine order. His presence preceded the work. This is always the pattern: the Spirit of the Lord arrives before the assignment is fulfilled.

When the Spirit came upon David in 1 Samuel 16:13, it was not merely for a task. It was for a relationship. David became a man after God's own heart because the Spirit of the Lord marked him for nearness, not just usefulness. When Jesus stood in the synagogue and read from Isaiah 61 — "The Spirit of the Lord is upon me" — He was declaring the source of everything He was about to do. The Spirit of the Lord did not give Jesus a strategy. He anointed Him with presence, and from that presence flowed every healing, every teaching, every miracle.

At Pentecost, the same Spirit fell on 120 people in an upper room and turned them into a movement that changed the world. They did not receive a plan. They received a Person.

The Spirit of the Lord is not one of God's tools. He is God's own presence. And when He rests upon you, everything else follows.
reflection:
Where in your life have you been reaching for strategy or strength before seeking the presence of God?

What would change in your daily routine if you treated the Spirit of the Lord as a Person to be with, not a power to be used?

In what area of your life do you sense God is commissioning you — and what does it look like to respond?
abide: Lord, before I ask You for anything else today, I ask simply for You. Let Your Spirit rest upon me. Where I have been trying to accomplish Your will in my own strength, forgive me and redirect me. I want Your presence more than Your power, knowing that when Your presence is real, everything else flows from it. Here I am.
